what_it_does: Documents three user-level systemd services that manage local vLLM serving daemons (chat, embedding, jina-v4) on ports 8000-8002, including unit patterns, lifecycle commands, boot survival via linger, failure modes, and open decisions.
observations: Three user-level systemd units are defined: vllm-chat.service, vllm-embed.service, and vllm-jina-v4.service.
Each service delegates to a shell launcher in ~/vllm-tests/ so launch logic can change without systemctl daemon-reload.
TimeoutStartSec=300 is set to accommodate torch.compile/CUDAGraphs warmup of roughly 150-180s.
Linger is not enabled as of 2026-05-27, so services die when the WSL user session ends.
Logging goes to the journal with no rotation policy and no service-level metrics are collected.
Open decisions include adding a fourth service, enabling linger, log rotation, metrics, a vllm-stack.target, and restart ordering.
standouts: Explicit separation of user-level vLLM services from the system-level ollama.service.
Launcher delegation pattern to avoid frequent systemctl daemon-reload.
Failure-mode table maps symptoms to likely causes and fixes, including CUDA toolchain drift and OOM.
conventions: Uses systemd user units with Restart=on-failure and RestartSec=10.
Wikilink cross-references to related operations, model, runtime, and toolchain docs.
Tables are used for state, lifecycle commands, and failure modes.
concerns: Linger not enabled means the documented survival across logout/login may not hold across a full WSL teardown.
No journal rotation policy is configured, so logs could grow unbounded.
No metrics or health checks beyond systemd restart-on-failure.
A potential VRAM conflict with a fourth service is noted but unresolved.
notes: This file is the operational runbook for starting, stopping, and troubleshooting the local vLLM systemd service stack.
questions: Is the intended audience Tim only, or should another operator be able to act without knowing the WSL/CUDA environment?
What exact commands does [[vllm-stack-cli]] provide and how do they differ from the listed systemctl commands?
Has the 300s TimeoutStartSec been validated against the actual cold-start duration for all three services?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to describe systemd services for three vLLM daemons; the content matches that purpose and adds lifecycle, failure-mode, and open-decision context.
apparent_themes: Operational reliability through systemd restart semantics and launcher delegation.
Explicit trade-off between resource consumption and service persistence (linger decision).
Local-first, manual operations on a personal WSL/CUDA workstation.
intention_signals: The file tries to turn an ad-hoc set of vLLM launch scripts into a maintainable, documented, auto-restarting service stack while leaving resource-persistence trade-offs as explicit owner decisions.
novelty: It treats a personal WSL GPU workstation with the same operational rigor (systemd units, failure-mode tables, open decisions) typically used for production server fleets.
contribution: A complete operational view of how the local vLLM daemons are kept alive, started, stopped, diagnosed, and what remains unresolved, which a fused system would need to preserve for operations and troubleshooting.
summary_for_embedding: Operations documentation for three user-level systemd services that run local vLLM chat and embedding daemons on a WSL/CUDA workstation, covering unit patterns, lifecycle commands, boot survival, failure modes, and open decisions.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a runbook for reliably managing and troubleshooting the local vLLM service stack under systemd.
suggested_fields: decision_owner — e.g., linger enablement is explicitly Tim's decision.
resource_budget — VRAM/RAM constraints that inform the fourth-service decision and gpu-memory-utilization values.
health_check_endpoint — whether each service exposes a readiness/health URL.
proposed_edge_kinds: delegates_to — between a service unit and its launcher script.
requires_decision — between a topic and an unresolved operational choice.
impacts_resource_usage — between a service and linger/GPU/RAM trade-offs.
